persons.arguments_against: No printed birth year (age gate unchecked) and only a single co-occurring edition (1883), below the strict corroboration bar. This fails decisively: MACKGLASHAN is a unique surname in the corpus — exactly 1 official in all staff lists and 1 bio — so there is no same-name relative or successor to confuse. The forename matches in full (John Charles / John C.), and the senior office matches verbatim: bio 'Auditor-general, Jamaica, April, 1875' against the staff list's 'Auditor-General' (Audit Office) 1877-1886. The bio's other roles also appear: 'clerk to privy council' -> 'Clerk to the Privy Council' (1877), 'member of legislative council, April, 1878' -> Auditor General listed under Legislative Council (1880). With a unique surname and an exact senior-post match there is no live counter-hypothesis.
persons.would_overturn: Evidence of a second Mackglashan in the Jamaica service — but the corpus shows only one.

notes: Unique surname in the corpus; full forename and the senior post Auditor-General Jamaica match exactly, with Privy Council and Legislative Council roles corroborating. Single edition co-occurrence is immaterial given uniqueness.
